root_text.an7.76:1.1: “Sattahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu vinayadharo hoti.
root_text.an7.76:1.7: ubhayāni kho panassa pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso,
root_text.an7.76:1.8: catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī,
root_text.an7.76:1.9: āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an7.76:1.10: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, sattahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu vinayadharo hotī”ti.

translation_text.an7.76:0.3: An Expert in the Monastic Law (2nd)
translation_text.an7.76:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with seven qualities is an expert in the monastic law.
translation_text.an7.76:1.3: They know what is an offense.
translation_text.an7.76:1.4: They know what is not an offense.
translation_text.an7.76:1.5: They know what is a light offense.
translation_text.an7.76:1.6: They know what is a serious offense.
translation_text.an7.76:1.7: Both monastic codes have been passed down to them in detail, well analyzed, well mastered, well evaluated in both the rules and accompanying material.
translation_text.an7.76:1.8: They get the four absorptions—blissful meditations in this life that belong to the higher mind—when they want, without trouble or difficulty.
translation_text.an7.76:1.9: They realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an7.76:1.10: A mendicant with these seven qualities is an expert in the monastic law.”
